name: Create and Manage an AKS Kubernetes Cluster
description: Provision a fully managed Kubernetes cluster on Azure Kubernetes Service, connect kubectl, and deploy a workload.
trendingReason: AKS adoption is surging as teams migrate from self-managed Kubernetes to managed offerings.

steps.title: Create a resource group
steps.description: Create a dedicated resource group for the AKS cluster.

steps.title: Create the AKS cluster
steps.command: az aks create --resource-group aks-rg --name myaks --node-count 2 --node-vm-size Standard_B2s --generate-ssh-keys --enable-managed-identity
steps.description: Provisions a two-node cluster with managed identity authentication.
steps.tip: Standard_B2s is cost-effective for dev/test. Use Standard_D4s_v5 or larger for production.
steps.warning: Cluster creation takes 5-10 minutes. Do not interrupt the command.

steps.title: Get cluster credentials
steps.description: Downloads the kubeconfig and merges it into your local kubectl context.

steps.title: Verify connectivity
steps.description: Confirms kubectl can reach the cluster and shows the node status.

steps.title: Deploy a sample workload
steps.command: kubectl create deployment nginx --image=nginx:latest --replicas=2 && kubectl expose deployment nginx --port=80 --type=LoadBalancer
steps.description: Deploys an nginx pod with two replicas and exposes it via an Azure Load Balancer.

steps.title: Scale the cluster
steps.description: Adds another node to the cluster to handle increased load.

fullScript:
# Create and manage an AKS cluster
az group create --name aks-rg --location eastus
az aks create --resource-group aks-rg --name myaks --node-count 2 --node-vm-size Standard_B2s --generate-ssh-keys --enable-managed-identity
az aks get-credentials --resource-group aks-rg --name myaks
kubectl get nodes
kubectl create deployment nginx --image=nginx:latest --replicas=2
kubectl expose deployment nginx --port=80 --type=LoadBalancer
az aks scale --resource-group aks-rg --name myaks --node-count 3

faq.question: How much does an AKS cluster cost?
faq.answer: The AKS control plane is free. You pay only for the underlying VMs, storage, and networking. A two-node Standard_B2s cluster costs roughly $50-70/month.

faq.question: How do I enable autoscaling on AKS?
faq.answer: Use 'az aks update --resource-group aks-rg --name myaks --enable-cluster-autoscaler --min-count 1 --max-count 5' to let the cluster scale nodes automatically based on pod demand.
